title: Replace Calibration-First Blending With a Direct AUC-Optimized OOF Meta-Learner
summary: The leaderboard metric is ROC AUC, so the final ensemble should optimize ranking directly, not just probability calibration.
rationale: The leaderboard metric is ROC AUC, so the final ensemble should optimize ranking directly, not just probability calibration. A pairwise AUC objective or listwise meta-learner on strictly OOF base predictions is a different lever from beta/isotonic calibration and may exploit complementary specialists better.
implementation_hint: Collect strictly OOF predictions from diverse specialists, then fit a simple linear or shallow tree meta-model with pairwise AUC loss or another rank-focused objective. Only after that consider a light monotone calibration if needed for stability, never using in-fold predictions.
expected_effect: Can improve public-score robustness when base models disagree on ordering near the pit window, even if each single model is already well calibrated.
risk: Pairwise meta-learning can overfit if too many highly correlated base models are included; any OOF mistake becomes instant leakage.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
